title: The Weatherman

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate theory about sleeve elastic: 'They call it an elastic cuff for a reason, 'cause it snaps back. You'd think it would, but it's not like a sock. A sock snaps back. You can wear a sock 500 times with no problem. No, socks are cheap, they don't snap back.'
jokes.setup: Larry refusing to roll up his sleeve for medical injection
jokes.punchline: His contradictory pseudo-expertise on elastic materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - turning minor inconvenience into elaborate conspiracy theory with false expertise

jokes.text: Dr. Funkhouser's meltdown: 'I have an hysteric on the phone, I have people in the waiting room, and this is enough already! It's enough.'
jokes.setup: Larry's elastic obsession delaying medical procedure
jokes.punchline: Dentist completely losing professional composure
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation payoff to Larry's absurd behavior

jokes.text: Good Hodgkin's vs bad Hodgkin's: 'Yeah, but it's the good Hodgkin's. I didn't know there was a good Hodgkin's. I'm not saying it's a great Hodgkin's. It's a good Hodgkin's.'
jokes.setup: Casual mention of uncle having Hodgkin's disease
jokes.punchline: Treating terminal illness like restaurant ratings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating cancer varieties like menu options with casual acceptance

jokes.text: Larry's sitting-down-to-pee explanation and his father's reaction: 'What are you, pussy-whipped? It's not really about her, it's about me. I pee sitting down.'
jokes.setup: Father assuming toilet seat complaint is about wife
jokes.punchline: Larry revealing his unconventional bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe setup - father's masculine assumption vs Larry's practical reality

jokes.text: Larry's rationalization: 'I was getting up to go at night sometimes and I didn't feel like putting the light on, so I would sit down, and then I got very comfortable with it.'
jokes.setup: Need to explain unconventional bathroom behavior
jokes.punchline: Logical progression from convenience to preference
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-rationalization of embarrassing behavior

jokes.text: Larry's Winston Churchill defense: 'Why stand when you can sit? Have you ever heard that expression? No, I never heard that. But I don't think he meant the toilet.'
jokes.setup: Larry invoking historical authority for bathroom habits
jokes.punchline: Father's logical objection to misapplied quote
jokes.explanation: Clever misappropriation of famous quote for absurd purpose

jokes.text: Larry: 'No matter where I sit at these dinner parties, it always seems like every conversation is more interesting than the one I've having.' Cheryl: 'Right. Except when you're sitting next to me.' Larry: 'No, it still feels that way.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl offering exception to Larry's complaint
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about finding her boring too
jokes.explanation: Perfect marriage comedy - the expected deflection followed by brutal honesty

jokes.text: Tooth photo reaction: Larry and Cheryl's horrified responses to the dental X-ray photo
jokes.setup: Opening mail containing dental photo
jokes.punchline: Their disgusted overreaction to seeing Larry's tooth
jokes.explanation: Setup for later payoff but weak on its own

jokes.text: Larry's translator wish: 'I like talking through a translator. It's fun, yeah. I wish I could do this with my wife.'
jokes.setup: Communicating with Russian cousin through translator
jokes.punchline: Wanting to use translator to talk to his own wife
jokes.explanation: Good marital observation - using language barrier as relationship buffer

jokes.text: Glasses-trying refusal escalating to physical grabbing: 'I don't really like people trying my glasses on... I'd rather not... no, no, nyet! No! What are you doing? No! Nyet!'
jokes.setup: Polite refusal to share glasses
jokes.punchline: Escalating to bilingual panic as Russian grabs for them
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Larry's boundary anxiety turning physical

jokes.text: Larry's increasingly frantic refusal to let Russian guest try on his glasses, escalating to shouting 'Nyet!'
jokes.setup: Russian guest wants to try Larry's glasses
jokes.punchline: Larry's neurotic refusal escalates to him shouting in Russian
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's personal space issues. The 'nyet' adds nice absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Larry's foot disgust: 'Great, I have to have dinner and look at his disgusting feet all night? They'll be under the table.'
jokes.setup: Russian guest going barefoot
jokes.punchline: Larry's disproportionate revulsion and Cheryl's logical response
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pet peeve blown out of proportion

jokes.text: Jeff's deadpan: 'Yeah, it's chock full of Funkhousers.'
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about lack of variety at dinner party
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact confirmation using 'chock full' phrase
jokes.explanation: Good supporting character moment with efficient wordplay

jokes.text: Sammi's response to Larry's shoe comment: 'Maybe you should take your shoes off like that guy. You could be twins. I don't want to be twins with him.'
jokes.setup: Child suggesting Larry emulate the Russian
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified rejection of the comparison
jokes.explanation: Child cutting through Larry's pretension with simple logic

jokes.text: Marty revealing Larry has plaque based on hygienist gossip, leading to Larry's outraged denial
jokes.setup: Marty casually mentions Larry's plaque problem
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror at the breach of dental confidentiality
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's privacy paranoia and social mortification. Sets up running gag.

jokes.text: Patient/hygienist confidentiality debate: Larry claiming ethical breach while others dismiss it
jokes.setup: Larry claiming privacy violation
jokes.punchline: Taking dental gossip as seriously as attorney-client privilege
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to legalize trivial social situations

jokes.text: Plaque contest challenge: 'You want to have a plaque contest? Anytime you want. Let's have a plaque contest.'
jokes.setup: Defending his dental hygiene
jokes.punchline: Turning dental care into competitive sport
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of dental hygiene into macho posturing

jokes.text: Tooth photo disaster at dinner party: child finding and showing the grotesque dental image
jokes.setup: Photo falls from Larry's jacket
jokes.punchline: Child displaying gruesome dental image to horrified dinner guests
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff creating social disaster

jokes.text: The tooth photo falling out of Larry's jacket at dinner party, causing mass disgust
jokes.setup: Earlier established the disgusting tooth photo
jokes.punchline: It appears at the worst possible moment - formal dinner
jokes.explanation: Solid callback with good timing. The social mortification is well-executed.

jokes.text: 'She could have told me where the sneaker was. It's not that hard. "In the closet," three words. One word..."closet." How about that?'
jokes.setup: Larry's frustrated about leaving without his sneaker
jokes.punchline: His mathematical breakdown of how easy the solution would have been
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-analysis of simple social interaction. The word count reduction is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Larry's prostitute analogy: 'It's like, if you go to a prostitute and the prostitute goes around telling everybody you got a small penis.'
jokes.setup: Explaining breach of dental hygienist confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Comparing dental gossip to sex worker discretion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analogy - perfect Larry logic connecting unrelated professional discretions

jokes.text: Larry's rapid disclaimers: 'Not that I've ever been to a prostitute. Not that I have a small penis.'
jokes.setup: Using prostitute analogy
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifying both aspects of his hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia about leaving wrong impression

jokes.text: Larry's price speculation: 'You plunk down $300 for a hooker, expect her to keep her mouth shut. I'm only guessing $300. What do I know?'
jokes.setup: Continuing prostitute discretion analogy
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by pricing speculation despite claiming no knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Larry can't help getting lost in tangential details

jokes.text: Larry falling in toilet because Cheryl left seat up after he taught her to keep it down
jokes.setup: Larry has been insisting on toilet seat protocol
jokes.punchline: He becomes victim of his own rule when Cheryl follows his sitting preference
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic payoff to earlier setup. Larry's own neurosis comes back to bite him - perfect structure.

jokes.text: Larry's weatherman conspiracy theory: 'What if the weatherman predicts it's gonna rain just so he can keep people off the golf course and have it for himself?'
jokes.setup: Clear day despite rain prediction
jokes.punchline: Paranoid theory about meteorologist golf conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoid logic - turning weather forecasting into personal vendetta

jokes.text: Golf pro casualness: 'Fell in the toilet.' when asked about the cane
jokes.setup: Chuck asking about Larry's injury
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact explanation of embarrassing accident
jokes.explanation: Simple callback with good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Larry confronting weatherman: 'I've got a sneaking suspicion that you're predicting rain to clear the golf course for yourself.'
jokes.setup: Finding weatherman golfing on supposedly rainy day
jokes.punchline: Direct accusation of meteorological fraud
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier paranoid theory with direct confrontation

jokes.text: Weatherman's scientific explanation vs Larry's dismissal: 'There's a jet stream of bullshit coming out of your mouth.'
jokes.setup: Weatherman giving scientific explanation involving jet streams
jokes.punchline: Larry twisting meteorological terms into insult
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning scientific terms into profane insult

jokes.text: Walter Brennan impression: 'Hey, Walter Brennan! The barn's on fire! The barn's on fire!'
jokes.setup: Larry walking away with cane
jokes.punchline: Random old movie reference mocking his gait
jokes.explanation: Obscure reference that may not land for modern audiences

jokes.text: Larry's sitting-urination reading habit: 'When you're peeing all over your shoe, I'm learning something. If I pee 20 times during a day, I get through the New York Times.'
jokes.setup: Defending sitting down to urinate
jokes.punchline: Turning bathroom time into educational opportunity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character logic - Larry finding intellectual justification for embarrassing habit

jokes.text: Jeff's confused response: 'You crap standing up?'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining sitting bathroom habits
jokes.punchline: Jeff's logical but absurd question about defecation positions
jokes.explanation: Good supporting character moment with logical absurdity

jokes.text: Oscar ate the sneaker: simple devastating punchline to Larry's retrieval attempt
jokes.setup: Larry waiting to get his sneaker back
jokes.punchline: Dog has completely destroyed it
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words destroy Larry's hopes

jokes.text: Name tag suede concern: Larry worrying about paper residue on his jacket
jokes.setup: Being asked to wear name tag
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over minor fabric damage
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with clothing obsessions

jokes.text: Plaque gossip spreads to strangers: woman at testimonial knowing about Larry's dental issues
jokes.setup: Random woman at event
jokes.punchline: She knows intimate details about Larry's dental hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of privacy paranoia theme

jokes.text: Leo Funkhouser testimonial speech interrupted by name tag disaster
jokes.setup: Emotional speech about father's legacy
jokes.punchline: Larry's name tag destroying his jacket during touching moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback timing during inappropriate moment

jokes.text: Russian's bald yarmulke insult: 'At least I don't hide my baldness with a yarmulke.'
jokes.setup: Argument escalating during testimonial
jokes.punchline: Larry making inappropriate religious/appearance attack
jokes.explanation: Offensive escalation showing Larry's worst impulses

jokes.text: Larry's back injury during standing ovation: unable to stand while everyone applauds
jokes.setup: Emotional climax of testimonial
jokes.punchline: Larry's toilet injury preventing him from showing respect
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback at worst possible moment

jokes.text: Final weatherman vindication: 'It had to freaking rain. It had to freaking rain!'
jokes.setup: Larry's entire conspiracy theory about fake weather
jokes.punchline: It actually rains, proving weatherman was right all along
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural irony - Larry's paranoia completely unfounded, universe mocking him

tmdb_overview: Larry David has a huge problem with name tags. In the process Larry reveals a bathroom preference that disgusts Jeff. Ted Danson makes another great cameo.
